# Chapter 2 Starting Machine Accounting

To log on and access Machine Accounting:

1\. Double-click the Machine Accounting icon on the desktop. The Machine Accounting
Logon window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/19.2.png)




2\. Enter a User Name and Password.

3\. Click Logon.

4\. If the Select a Date calendar appears, enter an audit date, or click the red X in the top-
right corner of the window to close it without entering an audit date.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/19.3.png)






![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/19.4.png)

Selecting an audit date is not necessary when configuring Machine Accounting.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="19" -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 2 Starting Machine Accounting" -->


## 2.1 Selecting the Audit Date

Upon successful log on, the user is prompted to select the accounting period (date).

To select an accounting period:

1\. From the Select a Date window, select a date on the calendar.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/20.1.png)




<table>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"></th>
<th>OK</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>4 February 2020</th>
<th>March 2020</th>
<th rowspan="2">Cancel</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>MTWTFSS</th>
<th>MTWTFSS</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>27 28 29 30 31 4 2</td>
<td>1</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3 4 5 6 7 8 9</td>
<td>2 3 4 5 6 7 8</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>10 11 12 13 14 15 46</td>
<td>9 10 11 12 13 14 15</td>
<td rowspan="2">StartOver</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>17 18 19 20 21 22 23</td>
<td>16 17 18 19 20 21 22</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">24 25 26 27 28 29</td>
<td>23 24 25 26 27 28 29</td>
<td>Re-Open</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3031 1 2 3 4 5</td>
<td>Key &gt;&gt;</td>
</tr>
</table>


2\. If needed, click Key to view the color and symbol codes:

· Red Square-Current date

· Green Text-Open accounting period

· Red Text-Closed accounting period

· Yellow Highlight-Current selection

· Black Text-Available dates




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/20.2.png)




3\. Click OK after selecting the applicable accounting period OR click Cancel to open a blank
Machine Accounting window. The MA User Interface appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="20" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 2 Starting Machine Accounting" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/21.1.png)




Additional options for the Select a Date window include the following:

· Select a closed date, then click OK to review the period (no updates allowed).

· Select a closed date, then click Re-Open to make updates to meters and actuals.

· Click Start Over to restart the accounting day and reset the period tasks if the day
does not open completely.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/21.2.png)




!
Clicking Start Over resets the selected accounting day AND all subsequent
days. Any previously completed work must be performed again.


## 2.2 Defining the Accounting Calendar

Use the Define Accounting Calendar option to configure the parameters of the accounting
calendar.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/21.3.png)




i
A warning notice appears when the accounting calendar is near its end date. The
end date is extendable for up to 10 years.

To define the accounting calendar:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Configuration > Define Accounting Calendar. The Define
Accounting Calendar window opens.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="21" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 2 Starting Machine Accounting" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/22.1.png)




2\. Set the following calendar parameters:

· Calendar Type

· Start Date (must be the first day of the fiscal year)

· End Of Week

· Years To Generate

· Days To Warn Before Calendar End

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="22" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 2 Starting Machine Accounting" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/23.1.png)




3\. Click Generate.

4\. To insert weeks, select an existing week, then click Insert Week.

5\. Click OK. The Define Accounting Calendar window closes, and the changes save.


## 2.3 Changing Passwords

Users may change their own password after successfully logging on.
To change a password:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Configuration > Change Password. The Change
Password window opens.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/23.2.png)




2\. Enter the old password in the Old Password field.

3\. Enter the new password in the New Password field.

4\. Re-enter the new password in the Confirm Password field.

5\. Click OK. The new password replaces the old password.
